steps.title: Identify the key signal in the research finding
steps.content: The research finding is that democratic strength, if associated with low inequality, restrains inflationary pressures. This directly contradicts the initial speculation prompted by the Mongolia-Cameroon comparison—that democracy inhibits inflation control.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A — it would be a mistake to treat the relative inflation rates of Mongolia and Cameroon as indicative of an inherent shortcoming in democratic institutions with regard to control over inflation. states directly that it would be a mistake to treat the Mongolia-Cameroon case as evidence that democracy inhibits inflation, which follows from the finding that democracy with low inequality actually restrains inflation.

The other three appeal to measurement error or mischaracterize the inequality relationship, sidestepping the core contradiction.

That matches Choice A — it would be a mistake to treat the relative inflation rates of Mongolia and Cameroon as indicative of an inherent shortcoming in democratic institutions with regard to control over inflation..
